description: A Japanese woodblock print (ukiyo-e) depicting a woman with long flowing black hair and pale skin holding a stout, plump-cheeked boy on her back. The boy holds a small golden axe or toy and has a determined expression. The woman gazes at him with an expression of maternal affection. Both figures are rendered with the characteristic fine lines and flat colors of the ukiyo-e tradition. Japanese calligraphic text and the artist's seal appear at the lower portion of the print.
